Editor Spokane Press In an edi-
torial in the Sunday Spokesman
Review the Industrial Workers of
the World are referred to as anar-
chistic: that they are hoboes and
loafers; that their fundamental
doctrine is the reputation of all
law and human authority. etc. OF
course the Review itself is the
authority" | do not belong to
the Industrial Workers of the
World. but l can say that the
charge against them in that par
ticular editorial Is false.


attended a meeting in their hall
Saturday evening. The hall was
filled with an orderly, clean, intel
ligent bunch of laboring men.
They are not broke, either. When
the collection was taken up they
all responded literally. The result
was nearly hundred dollars.


The speakers declared they
stood for law and order and not
against it. 1.-THE constitution of
the United States Is good enough
for me-" said the chief speaker,
and l am ready to die for it." OF
course there are some foreigners
among them. What of 1t? Are
there not foreigners on the police
force? If 1t is crime to be for
tigner, is 1t not something of a
crime to be a descendant of for
eigner?


This thing of domineering or
lauding 1t over laboring men is
getting stale. The Review seems
to imagine itself the great and
only thing with a right to exist.
hope the people Of Spokane
will waken to the fact that they
can conduct the affairs of the city
without being bossed by that
bunch on the corner of Riverside
avenue and Monroe street.


These men want nothing more
than their equal rights. Those con-
fined in jail have committed no
crime. Many Of them have fami-
lies and have no time tc spend In
Jail. Very truly yours.


f. III. SANDERS,
WHO'S Fourth Avenue.



Editor The Spokane Press: The
present situation in Spokane is
disgraceful. l am visitor to Spo-
kane. One evening, walking on
the main street. | was dubbed In
the back by policeman and near
ly arrested because did not walk
fast enough, and another evening
saw the fire department, with
hoses attched to the mains. soaking
men and women for not scattering
and leaving the premises, simply
because the |. w. w. demands lib
erty and free speech as upheld by
bhe constitution of the United
States. have been through Alas
ka and the United States. Also in
Seattle during the I. w. w. fight
for free speech, and saw many con-
